title: jasminum floridum subsp. giraldii
intro: Jasminum floridum subsp. giraldii, also known as Girald's jasmine, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the olive family that is native to China and Southeast Asia. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, as well as reduce fever and alleviate headaches. The health benefits of Girald's jasmine include its ability to promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve sleep quality, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Girald's jasmine is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
